titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Jean M. Conklin Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Alicia Detelich

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday November 14, 2020 at 09:58

did.abstract: The personal and family papers of Jean M. Conklin, Yale University employee and wife of Yale anthropology professor Harold C. Conklin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Jean M. Conklin papers document the life of a Japanese American family that settled initially in San Francisco, California and later in Sharon, Pennsylvania. Included in the collection are materials created and collected by Jean Conklin about the Morisuye (Jean's father's line) and Hasegawa (Jean's mother's line) families, of which Jean was among the first generation born in the United States. Also included among the material is documentation about Jean's father, Masanobu Moriuye, and his efforts to establish the first Japanese American Boy Scout troop in San Franciso, as well as materials documenting Jean's childhood, college years, and life as the wife of Yale anthropology professor Harold C. Conklin. Also included are pieces of Jean's artwork, Jean's public school and college papers, family correspondence, family scrapbooks, 8mm-film footage of family travel between the 1930s and 1960s, VHS tapes, photographs, slides, and research materials related to her family's genealogy.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The bulk of the materials is open for research. Series VII, Yale University, contains personnel records and is closed until 1 January 2071 by Yale University policy. Contact Public Services for information on requesting access to restricted university records.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Jean Mieko Conklin was born in Sharon, Pennsylvania on August 26, 1926 to Masanobu Morisuye and Kikue Hasegawa Morisuye, Japanese immigrants. Masanobu, Jean's father, was an engineer at the Westinghouse Corporation and founded the first Japanese American Boy Scout troop in the United States. Jean graduated from Oberlin College in 1948 and earned a master's degree in biology from Brown University in 1950. After graduating from Oberlin, Jean married Yale University anthropology professor Harold C. Conklin and accompanied him as a research assistant on several of his anthropological field trips. Jean worked at Yale University in a variety of roles, serving in several positions at the Yale School of Medicine, the Human Resources department, the Office of the General Counsel, and the Athletic Director's office. Her avocational interests included family genealogy, which is documented extensively in her papers, as well as photography, writing, and travel. Jean died in Hamden, Connecticut on July 22, 2010 at the age of 83.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has been transferred to Yale University for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by the creator(s) of this collection. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged in six series: I. Correspondence, 1940-2006. II. Family Papers, 1915-2005. III. School Papers, Notebooks, and Diaries, 1930-2004. IV. Travel Papers, 1952-2003. V. Scrapbooks and Photographs, 1890-1980. VI. Audiovisual Materials, 1930-1999.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of the estate of Harold C. Conklin, 2016.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Jean M. Conklin Papers (MS 2057). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The bulk of the materials was arranged by the donor. Information included in the Scope and Contents note and the collection inventory is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from a survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the collection inventory are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles were supplied by staff during processing.

corpname.part: Yale University. Office of the General Counsel

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series primarily consists of incoming and outgoing correspondence between Jean Conklin and members of her immediate and extended family during the mid-to-late 20th century. Some correspondence between Jean and Harold (Hal) Conklin and their friends is also included. Topics include family history, family events, and accounts of trips taken by Jean and Hal Conklin as part of Hal Conklin's anthropological research. Occassionally related materials are included with the correspondence.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged as received from the donor.

did.unittitle: Arai, Fujiko, family correspondence and notes
Morisue, Jo, correspondence and Word War II FBI relocation permit materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes family trees for the Morisuye and Conklin families.

did.unittitle: Family correspondence of Jean Conklin, Kikue Hasegawa Morisuye, Masanobu Morisuye, and Eleanor Morisuye Masae
Letters to Jean and Hal Conklin, birth of Bruce Conklin, and Morisuye trip
Kikue Hasegawa Morisuye, correspondence and memorabilia
Condolence letters for Masae Morisuye's passing
Condolence letters for Kikue Hasegawa Morisuye's passing
Barrio Council of Kinakin irrigation petition

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of genealogical and other materials related to the Morisuye and Hasegawa families. Materials found in this series include genealogical charts, family history narratives, news articles, correspondence, photographs, and notes.

Also included in this series are materials created and collected by Jean's parents, including documentation relating to Masanobu Morisuye's efforts to establish a Japanese American Boy Scout troop in San Francisco and his work as an engineer, as well as notebooks, drawings, and sketches created by Kikue Hasegawa Morisuye.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged as received from the donor.

did.unittitle: Japanese translation by Syuziro Uziie and Yoshiko Bhueler
Ginnosuke Hasegawa and family, photographs and anecdotes
Bruce Conklin term paper on Morisuye family history
Hasegawa and Morisuye family genealogies and anecdotes
Notes on contents of two Morisuye family films
Jean Conklin retirement from Yale Human Resources and social events
International Executive Service Corps documents and photographs
Westinghouse retirement letters and dinner program
Writings on Cha-no-yu, Sumi, and festivals

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of materials related to Jean's high school years and her time as an undergraduate at Oberlin College. Materials include sketches, notebooks, diaries, scrapbooks, and assorted high school, college, and church papers.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged as received from the donor.

did.unittitle: Oberlin commencement program, bulletin, and handbooks

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of notes and correspondence related to the extensive personal and professional travels of the Morisuye family and of Hal and Jean Conklin.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged as received from the donor.

did.unittitle: Japan and around the world trip
Morisuye family vacation to San Francisco

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes scrapbooks, photographs, and slides documenting the early life, genealogy, and travels of Jean Conklin and her family. Many of the photographs and slides were taken by Masanobu Morisuye, who was an amateur photographer.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged as received from the donor.

did.unittitle: Hasegawa and Morisuye family photographs, copies
Masanobu Morisuye, class photographs and illustration clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series is comprised of 8mm films and VHS tapes documenting the early life and travels of Jean Conklin and her family. Some commercially produced 8mm film reels acquired by Jean during her travels are also included.

did.unittitle: Jean and Eleanor with ducks at Buhl Farm
Bruce in playpen at 70 Morningside Drive
Km on Columbia campus, with Bruce
Katsanori's service and Kosaka Nao's service
Jean D reels: Jean, Hal, Bruce, Mark
Jean's comments on two Morisuye videos, with transcript
